narrative:
DOMINANT DRIVERS

1. USD weakness because DXY down 0.34%, CSM shows USD at zero strength on both H4 and D1, NFP wage softness reinforces Fed pivot expectations
2. CHF and NZD outperformance because CHF scores 100 on D1 CSM and H4 CSM simultaneously while gold rallying confirms safe haven demand layered with risk-on commodity bid
3. H4 risk-on momentum diverging from D1 mixed because EUR and GBP accelerating sharply on H4 versus neutral D1 signals, copper up 3% and S&P up 0.84% pulling carry and growth currencies higher

PRICE CONFIRMS

EURUSD H4 buy signal at plus 4 and USDCHF H4 strong sell at minus 4 are moving in lockstep with their negative 0.90 correlation, confirming the anti-USD theme is genuine and not noise. GBPUSD and NZDUSD are both confirming with their positive 0.84 to 0.93 correlations all aligned directionally against the dollar. CADJPY at minus 4 sell signal with Canada jobs disappointing confirms CAD is the weakest high-beta currency and is lagging the broader risk-on impulse seen in AUDJPY and EURJPY.

REGIME

Mixed conflicted, VIX rising 1.58% and gold up 0.64% to 4730 signal residual safe haven demand while S&P 500 at 7399 up 0.84% and copper up nearly 3% argue risk-on, making this a USD-off regime rather than a clean risk-on or risk-off environment.

CONTRADICTIONS

1. Risk sentiment label versus price action: news flags risk-off but copper up 3%, S&P up 0.84%, and H4 CSM regime reads risk-on medium, correlation and price override the narrative label
2. NZD divergence: D1 CSM shows NZD at 99 strength but H4 CSM has NZD fading to 56, creating a timeframe conflict that undermines conviction on NZD longs despite strong signals elsewhere

TRADE IMPLICATIONS

Best: USDCHF down because CHF 100 on both timeframes, USD at zero, H4 strong sell confirmed, conviction 8/10
Secondary: EURUSD up because EUR accelerating on H4 CSM from 39 to 80, USD structurally weak, H4 buy plus 4, conviction 7/10
Avoid: NZDUSD because NZD CSM divergence between D1 and H4 creates conflicting timeframe signals
Watch: DXY breaking below 97.50 confirms primary USDCHF trade and validates the H4 risk-on regime completing a D1 transition

themes.theme: US Jobs Data Mixed - NFP Beat but Wage Growth Softens
Middle East Tensions and Iran Hostilities Escalating
Safe Haven Demand - Gold and Silver Rally
Fed Policy Shift to Inflation Focus from Labor Market
Canada Jobs Disappoint - Weakness in Labor Market
Eurozone Economic Weakness - Industrial Slump and War Inflation
US Consumer Sentiment Deteriorates Below Expectations
Risk-Off Market Sentiment - Equities and Risk Assets Pressure

key_observation: USD weakening despite NFP beat due to soft wage growth, deteriorating consumer sentiment, and safe haven flows into gold. Middle East escalation and doubts about US-Iran peace deal creating geopolitical risk premium. Fed policy expectations shifting from labor focus to inflation concerns amid European war-driven price pressures.
watch: Iran response timing and escalation path in Strait of Hormuz. University of Michigan sentiment miss signals consumer confidence erosion. ECB inflation guidance versus Fed rate path divergence. CAD weakness may accelerate if labor market deterioration confirmed.
